consultations.notes: Rest for 5 days. Hot fomentation. Avoid heavy lifting.

Mix with small amount of food

prescriptions.instructions: Directly Observed Treatment - ASHA to supervise

consultations.chiefComplaint: Chest tightness on exertion, breathlessness climbing stairs, fatigue since 2 weeks

labTests.result: ST depression in leads V4-V6, T-wave inversion in V5-V6
labTests.impression: Changes suggestive of anterolateral ischaemia. Recommend urgent cardiology referral.
Severely elevated LDL and triglycerides. HDL low. High cardiovascular risk.
Poorly controlled diabetes over past 3 months.
Mildly elevated. Early diabetic nephropathy suspected. Monitor.

referral.reason: ECG changes suggestive of ischaemia. 2D Echo and Treadmill Test recommended.

consultations.notes: High-risk patient. Strict diabetic and salt-restricted diet. No heavy farm work until cardiac evaluation. Abstain from smoking/alcohol.

consultations.chiefComplaint: Irregular periods for 6 months, weight gain despite eating less, excess facial hair, hair fall

prescriptions.instructions: With meals. For insulin resistance in PCOS.
Empty stomach, 30 min before breakfast. Do not take with milk/antacids.

result.rightOvary: Enlarged 4.2 x 3.1 cm, 14 follicles seen (pearl necklace sign)

labTests.impression: Bilateral polycystic ovaries. Consistent with PCOS.
TSH elevated - Subclinical hypothyroidism. T3/T4 within normal limits.
Elevated LH:FSH ratio (>2) consistent with PCOS. Borderline testosterone.

consultations.notes: Low GI diet, exercise 45 min/day. Repeat TFT and USG at 3 months. Counselled on fertility implications. No current desire for pregnancy - management focused on cycle regulation.

consultations.chiefComplaint: Swelling in legs and feet, reduced urine output, extreme tiredness, nausea in mornings

prescriptions.instructions: Take in morning only. Monitor urine output.

labTests.impression: eGFR 32 mL/min - CKD Stage 3b. Elevated creatinine and BUN. Mild hyperkalaemia. Metabolic acidosis.
Significant proteinuria and granular casts - consistent with diabetic nephropathy.
Moderate normocytic normochromic anaemia. Likely renal anaemia due to CKD.
Bilateral small kidneys with increased echogenicity - consistent with chronic kidney disease.

referral.reason: CKD Stage 3b with progression. Dialysis planning may be required.

consultations.notes: Strict low-protein, low-potassium, low-salt diet. NO NSAIDs ever. Fluid restriction 1.5L/day. Family counselled about CKD progression and possible dialysis in future.

consultations.chiefComplaint: Sudden episode of severe breathlessness at night, wheezing, unable to complete sentences

prescriptions.frequency: every 20 minutes x 3 doses, then as needed
prescriptions.instructions: Use nebuliser. Keep child calm. Sit upright.
After breakfast. Do not stop abruptly.
Use spacer. Rinse mouth after use.

labTests.result: Hyperinflated lung fields. No consolidation or pleural effusion. Flattened diaphragm.
labTests.impression: Features consistent with acute asthma. No pneumonia.
Hypoxaemia with mild hypercapnia. Respiratory acidosis. Severe attack requiring immediate treatment.

consultations.notes: Emergency management initiated. SpO2 improved to 96% after 3 nebulisations. Advised admission but family refused. Parents educated on asthma triggers - dust, smoke, cold air. Asthma action plan given. Return IMMEDIATELY if breathing worsens.

disabilities: Right hand index finger amputation (occupational injury 2019)

consultations.chiefComplaint: High fever with chills for 5 days, severe headache, body ache, yellow eyes noticed by wife

prescriptions.instructions: Administered at hospital. Patient admitted in OPD ward.
For fever. Do not exceed 4 tablets/day.

labTests.impression: Significant titres for S. typhi O and H antigens. Consistent with active Enteric Fever (Typhoid).
Elevated bilirubin and transaminases. Reactive hepatitis secondary to typhoid. No features of fulminant hepatic failure.
Leucopenia (low WBC) - classic finding in typhoid. Thrombocytopenia - monitor closely for bleeding.

labTests.result: Salmonella typhi isolated. Sensitive to Ceftriaxone, Ciprofloxacin. Resistant to Ampicillin.
labTests.impression: Confirmed Typhoid. Use Ceftriaxone as per sensitivity.

consultations.notes: Patient responding to IV Ceftriaxone. Fever defervescing. Soft liquid diet only. Strict hand hygiene and sanitation. Family members advised OPD visit if they develop fever. Water purification advised - boil drinking water.

consultations.chiefComplaint: Fatigue, increased thirst, frequent urination, joint pains, cold intolerance, constipation for past 3 months

prescriptions.instructions: Take 15–30 min before first meal of the day.
Empty stomach, 30 min before breakfast. Do not take with milk or antacids.
Take with full glass of water. Sit upright for 30 min after. Do not eat for 30 min.

labTests.impression: Suboptimally controlled diabetes. Intensification of therapy required.
Elevated TSH (7.2) with low-normal FT4. Under-replaced hypothyroidism. Levothyroxine dose adjustment required.
T-scores < -2.5 at both lumbar spine (-2.8) and left hip (-2.6). Consistent with OSTEOPOROSIS. High fracture risk. Anti-resorptive therapy (Alendronate) initiated.

consultations.notes: Patient is 63-year-old post-menopausal woman managing 4 chronic conditions. Counselled on diet (low sugar, low salt, high calcium), weight reduction, and daily 30-min walk. ABHA card verified and records updated. Tele-consultation conducted successfully via ASHA worker assistance.
